label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. So, the story is about Mr. Williams who was driving and encountered a "SLOW" sign at a crossroad. He slowed down, checked both ways, and then drove across without stopping. Then a policeman stopped him, saying he didn't stop at the crossing. Mr. Williams explained that the sign said "SLOW," not "STOP," and he did slow down. The policeman then realized he was in the wrong street. The question is asking what Mr. Williams was doing when he was stopped by the policeman. The options are: A) was driving at a high speed B) was driving onto the main road C) was going to stop his car D) was driving slowly Let me recall the details. The key part is when Mr. Williams approached the crossroad with a "SLOW" sign. He slowed down, looked both ways, and then drove across without stopping. So when he was stopped by the policeman, he was in the process of driving across the main road, having already slowed down. The policeman's issue was that he didn't stop, but the sign only said "SLOW," so Mr. Williams was correct in slowing but not stopping.
